name: mostSyncAlloc
title: mostSyncAlloc
syntax: Callback: void OnMostSyncAllocResult(long allocResult, long numChannels, long channels[]);
description: This function reserves synchronous bandwidth by sending an Alloc system message to the MOST TimingMaster.

The result is reported by means of the callback function OnMostSyncAllocResult(). This requires defining OnMostSyncAllocResult() in the CAPL program using the following signature: OnMostSyncAllocResult(long allocResult, long numChannels, long channels[])
This function is called on completion of the allocation of channels triggered by the call of the function.
Whether the allocation was successful and if so, which channels were allocated, is indicated in the parameters described below.

parameters.name: numChannels
parameters.description: The number of channels to be reserved: MOST25: 1 <= numChannels <= 8 MOST150: 1 <= numChannels <= MaxSyncBandwidth (depends on boundary settings - max. value: 372)

parameters.name: kBusy = 0x02
parameters.description: The channels were not reserved, because the TimingMaster is busy processing another request

parameters.name: kDeny = 0x03
parameters.description: The channels were not reserved, because there are no more channels available

parameters.name: kWrong = 0x04
parameters.description: The reservation request contains invalid parameters

parameters.name: kUnknown = 0xFF
parameters.description: Unspecified error (e.g. a timeout while waiting for the TimingMaster to respond)

parameters.name: numChannels
parameters.description: The number of reserved channels. (1 <= numChannels <= 8).

parameters.name: channels[]
parameters.description: The values contained in this parameter are valid for allocResult = 0x01 only. It passes numChannels channel numbers reserved via mostSyncAlloc(). The value in channels[0] designates the label used for administration of the reserved channels.

availability: MOST25 MOST150 After measurement start Not in Stopmeasurement
